title: Kuma’s Life as a Buccaneer
description: On pages 5–7, this part focuses on Unknown person #4, York, Ivankov, Dragon, Unknown person #5, Bonney as Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.
scene: Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm glad you care about me, really... but you shouldn't come when I'm like this.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Kumachi has a big heart... Please take care of him for me.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: There's only one place she'd go now!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And kumachi, I just want you to know...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I-it happened when she was exposed to natural light...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: It turned all blue in the sun...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: What could've done this to her...?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: She burned up her remaining time, exposing herself to the sun,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: So she could cross the ocean...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: And make it home to us...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Because she wanted to ensure her baby's safety.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I swear... I'll raise her well.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: You're tilting the bottle too much!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Bonney-chan still looks uncomfortable! I'll show you how it's done!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And if you're gonna be sleeping next to her. Make sure her cot is like an iron cage!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We can't have you smashing her in your sleep with that massive body!!

summary: This part advances the Kuma and Bonney’s Tragedy through 43 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
